findings.title: Documentation drift: STATUS/ROADMAP claim user-agnostic refactor pending; commit e6257aa shows it complete

snapshot_evidence.evidence: imported script still carries two WYN OPS accent comments (lines 17 and 35); user-agnostic refactor is the next change
User-agnostic refactor commit — replace WYN OPS accent comments with neutral wording
Phase 1 — User-Agnostic Refactor ... Replace WYN OPS accent comments with neutral language
#   - Title + action footer styled with a single accent color.
H1=$'\e[1;38;5;208m'      # lesson title — bold orange accent
e6257aa refactor: remove user-specific accent branding from tutor

findings.recommendation_boundary: Update STATUS.md (lines 33-34, 41-46) and ROADMAP.md (Phase 1, lines 30-46) to reflect that commit e6257aa has performed the refactor. Scope: 2 documentation files; no script changes.

findings.title: Welcome-screen sandbox-safety claim broader than the practice subshell actually sandboxes

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Everything happens in ~/.shelltutor; you cannot break anything
practice() spawns bash --rcfile <(cat <<RCFILE ... cd $SANDBOX; HISTFILE=...; PS1=...; next/prev/show/quit/exit overrides
Do not introduce dependencies that require installation outside bash and a standard POSIX userland

findings.recommendation_boundary: Narrow the welcome-screen text at shelltutor:169 to match implementation. The actual sandboxing is (1) cd $SANDBOX inside the practice subshell, and (2) HISTFILE sandboxing. Strengthening enforcement (bwrap/firejail/chroot) violates AGENTS.md:75-78. Remediation is text-only; manual-review-only fitness function. Scope: 1 line.

findings.title: Lesson portability gaps: Lesson 7 (/proc, free -h) and Lesson 8 (sudo dnf install) use platform-specific surfaces without gate-and-fallback

snapshot_evidence.evidence: POSIX-ish terminal (any modern Linux or macOS terminal works) ... Stay portable across Linux and macOS
Lessons must be runnable end-to-end on a clean Linux or macOS terminal
If a lesson genuinely requires a non-portable surface, gate it on a runtime check and degrade gracefully
free -h ... cat /proc/cpuinfo ... cat /proc/meminfo ... ls /proc
/proc is Linux's self-report folder
Skip any that says 'command not found'. To install: sudo dnf install cowsay figlet lolcat

findings.recommendation_boundary: Either (a) apply CONTRIBUTING.md:51-54 gate-and-fallback to lesson 7's /proc and free references and lesson 8's dnf install hint, or (b) narrow the portability claim. Scope: ~30 lines of lesson heredoc OR three lines of governance text.

findings.title: Safety policy rules in AGENTS.md have no code-level enforcement

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Do not introduce dependencies that require installation outside bash and a standard POSIX userland
tutor must not write outside its own working directory, must not request elevated privileges, and must not reach the network
local commit discipline is the enforcement layer
no project-tracked git hooks; nothing in .githooks/ or .github/workflows/

findings.recommendation_boundary: Add FF-001 (safety-rule static analysis) and FF-002 (write-scope static analysis). Implementation does not require CI activation; pre-commit hook OR Makefile target OR manual-review checklist documented in CONTRIBUTING.md suffices. Scope: one new script or one section in CONTRIBUTING.md.

findings.title: AGENTS.md:22 cites parent workspace governance by absolute machine-local path

snapshot_evidence.evidence: The Jefahnierocks workspace contract at /Users/verlyn13/Organizations/jefahnierocks/AGENTS.md also applies inside this repository.
Rules in that file and rules restated here are stated on shelltutor's own authority

findings.recommendation_boundary: Replace the absolute path in AGENTS.md:22 with a relative path expression (../AGENTS.md) or a phrase without a filesystem citation. The rules are restated locally on lines 24-30; the absolute path is informational. Classification: cosmetic-or-framing-deviation (§9.6). Scope: 1 line.

findings.title: No automated eval / smoke / regression coverage for any path

snapshot_evidence.evidence: find . -name '*test*' -o -name '*spec*' -o -name '*eval*' returns only directive set + profile snapshot (audit infrastructure)
Phase 2 — Portability Validation ... Add a shellcheck pass; resolve or annotate findings
Walk the affected lesson surface ... If shellcheck is available, run it

findings.recommendation_boundary: Either (a) implement FF-005 (shellcheck shelltutor clean) and FF-006 (lesson-flow smoke test), or (b) execute ROADMAP Phase 2 manual walkthrough on macOS + Linux and record the result in STATUS.md. Both are valid; the latter is consistent with the workspace's current 'norms-based' enforcement layer. Scope: new test infrastructure OR a STATUS.md update with date-stamped validation.

findings.title: De-facto external contracts (CLI args, exit codes, env vars, sandbox layout) lack formal schemas

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Six contracts (C-001..C-006) all classified 'ad-hoc'
usage() heredoc — the only place the CLI shape is enumerated

findings.recommendation_boundary: Not actionable at current scope. Document the four de-facto contracts (CLI args, exit codes, env vars, sandbox layout) in a 'Contracts' section of README.md or a new docs/contracts.md. No machine-readable schema warranted for a single-file CLI with no public SDK. Promote to formal schema when release posture closes (ROADMAP Phase 4) or when SHELLTUTOR_HOME becomes a documented integration point.

priority_ranking_note: Three findings tied at priority 3.0 (F-001, F-002, F-003). Within the tie they are listed by chronological audit-attention order (the order in which evidence accumulated). The operator may resolve the tie by remediation order at planning time.
